target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $5.6B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

data.narrative:
Nominal, a Series B AI & Software company, is valued at $1.0B post-money following $155M in total fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 9 private-stage companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), including peers such as Figure AI ($39.0B Series C), Skild AI ($14.0B Series C), and Physical Intelligence ($5.6B Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $1.0B last post-money valuation and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implies a value of $1.9B. The peer-median method, based on three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, yields a median valuation of $14.0B, with a range spanning $5.6B to $39.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business maturity and investor demand within the frontier AI segment.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $1.6B to $16.1B, with a median of $8.0B. This wide range reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate pricing relative to high-growth peer outcomes. The stage-progression method suggests conservative positioning versus median peers, while peer-median anchoring reflects current market pricing for comparable-stage AI companies. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory, technical differentiation relative to the peer set, and capital efficiency relative to comparables.
